monday.question: What does the word 'experience' mean?
options: Something that happens to you and teaches you something
A plan you make for the future
A mistake you never repeat
A place you want to visit
monday.correctAnswer: Something that happens to you and teaches you something

monday.question: What does 'ever' mean in 'Have you ever tried Japanese food?'
options: At any point in your life
Only recently
Every single day
A very long time ago
monday.correctAnswer: At any point in your life

monday.question: What does 'never' mean in a Present Perfect sentence?
options: Not at any time in your life
Not today
Not very often
Not recently
monday.correctAnswer: Not at any time in your life

monday.question: What does 'just' mean in 'I have just arrived'?
options: A very short time ago
A long time ago
Not yet
Many times before

monday.question: What does 'recently' mean?
options: Not long ago, in the near past
A very long time ago
In the future
Every day
monday.correctAnswer: Not long ago, in the near past

monday.question: What does 'so far' mean in 'I have visited three countries so far'?
options: Up to this point in time
Very far from here
A long time ago
At the end
monday.correctAnswer: Up to this point in time

monday.question: What does 'up to now' mean?
options: Until the present moment
Very soon
At any time
Only once

monday.question: Which sentence uses the Present Perfect correctly to talk about a life experience?
options: I have been to Paris.
I was been to Paris.
I have went to Paris.
I did go to Paris.

monday.question: Which question correctly asks about a life experience?
options: Have you ever tried surfing?
Did you ever have tried surfing?
Have you ever try surfing?
Did you have ever tried surfing?

monday.question: Which is the correct short answer to 'Have you ever lived abroad?'
options: Yes, I have.
Yes, I did.
Yes, I was.
Yes, I do.

monday.question: Have you _____ climbed a mountain?
She has _____ tried skydiving. She's too afraid.
I have _____ (see) that movie. It was amazing!
They have _____ (travel) to more than ten countries.
My sister _____ a horse before. It was her first time.
_____ you ever _____ a famous person?
Translate to English: 'Você já experimentou comida indiana?'
monday.correctAnswer: have you ever tried indian food?

monday.question: Translate to English: 'Ele nunca andou de moto.'
monday.correctAnswer: he has never ridden a motorcycle

monday.question: Translate to English: 'Eu já fui ao Rio de Janeiro.'
monday.correctAnswer: i have been to rio de janeiro|i have already been to rio de janeiro

monday.question: Which sentence is wrong?
options: She has never eaten sushi.
Have you ever met a celebrity?
I have never been in Tokyo.
He has ever tried bungee jumping.
monday.correctAnswer: He has ever tried bungee jumping.

tuesday.question: What does 'already' express in a sentence like 'I have already eaten'?
options: Something happened before expected or before now
Something has not happened yet
Something happened a very long time ago
Something will happen soon
tuesday.correctAnswer: Something happened before expected or before now

tuesday.question: What does 'yet' express in 'I haven't finished yet'?
options: Something expected hasn't happened up to now
Something happened earlier than expected
Something that will never happen
Something that happened just now
tuesday.correctAnswer: Something expected hasn't happened up to now

tuesday.question: Where does 'already' usually go in an affirmative sentence?
options: Between 'have/has' and the past participle
At the beginning of the sentence
At the end of the sentence only
After the subject

tuesday.question: Where does 'yet' usually go in a negative sentence?
options: At the end of the sentence
Between 'haven't' and the past participle
At the beginning of the sentence
After the subject
tuesday.correctAnswer: At the end of the sentence

tuesday.question: Which sentence uses 'already' correctly?
options: I have already read that book.
I already have read that book.
I have read already that book.
Already I have read that book.
tuesday.correctAnswer: I have already read that book.

tuesday.question: Which sentence uses 'yet' correctly?
options: She hasn't called me yet.
She yet hasn't called me.
She hasn't yet me called.
She has called me yet.

tuesday.question: Which sentence uses 'just' correctly?
options: He has just finished his homework.
He just has finished his homework.
He has finished just his homework.
Just he has finished his homework.
tuesday.correctAnswer: He has just finished his homework.

tuesday.question: Which negative sentence is correct?
options: They haven't decided yet.
They didn't have decided yet.
They have not decided already.
They haven't decide yet.

tuesday.question: I have _____ seen this film. Let's watch something else.
Have you packed your bags _____?
She has _____ arrived. She's still on the way.
He has _____ (just) _____ (finish) the report.
We haven't booked the hotel _____.
Don't tell me the ending! I _____ that series.
You don't need to remind him. He _____ about the meeting.
Translate to English: 'Eu já terminei minha lição de casa.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: i have already finished my homework|i've already finished my homework

tuesday.question: Translate to English: 'Ela ainda não respondeu o email.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: she hasn't replied to the email yet|she has not replied to the email yet

tuesday.question: Translate to English: 'Eles acabaram de chegar.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: they have just arrived|they've just arrived

tuesday.question: Translate to English: 'Você já comprou os ingressos?'
tuesday.correctAnswer: have you already bought the tickets?|have you bought the tickets yet?

tuesday.question: Which sentence is wrong?
options: I have already eaten lunch.
She hasn't called me yet.
Have you already finished?
He has yet arrived.

wednesday.question: When do we use the Simple Past instead of the Present Perfect?
options: When we mention a specific time in the past
When we talk about life experiences in general
When the action is still relevant now
When we use 'ever' or 'never'
wednesday.correctAnswer: When we mention a specific time in the past

wednesday.question: Which sentence uses the correct tense?
options: I visited Rome last summer.
I have visited Rome last summer.
I have visited Rome in 2019.
I did visit Rome last summer.
wednesday.correctAnswer: I visited Rome last summer.

wednesday.question: Which sentence uses the correct tense?
options: She has tried sushi once in her life.
She tried sushi once in her life.
She tried sushi last week.
She has tried sushi last week.
wednesday.correctAnswer: She has tried sushi once in her life.

wednesday.question: Choose the correct tense: 'I _____ that museum in 2021.'
options: visited
have visited
have been visiting
was visiting
wednesday.correctAnswer: visited

wednesday.question: Choose the correct tense: 'I _____ that museum, but I don't remember when.'
options: have visited
visited
was visiting
did visit
wednesday.correctAnswer: have visited

wednesday.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Did you ever try bungee jumping?
Have you ever tried bungee jumping?
Have you ever try bungee jumping?
Did you have ever tried bungee jumping?
wednesday.correctAnswer: Have you ever tried bungee jumping?

wednesday.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: When did you go to London?
When have you gone to London?
When have you been to London?
When did you been to London?
wednesday.correctAnswer: When did you go to London?

wednesday.question: Someone says 'I have been to Japan.' You want to know WHEN. What do you ask?
options: When did you go?
When have you gone?
When were you going?
When did you been?
wednesday.correctAnswer: When did you go?

wednesday.question: I _____ (see) that movie last night. It was fantastic.
wednesday.correctAnswer: saw

wednesday.question: She _____ (live) in Canada for two years when she was a child.
wednesday.correctAnswer: lived

wednesday.question: He _____ (never / try) Thai food. He should give it a chance.
wednesday.correctAnswer: has never tried

wednesday.question: They _____ (meet) for the first time at a conference in 2020.
wednesday.correctAnswer: met

wednesday.question: A: Have you ever been to Florianópolis? B: Yes! I _____ there in 2022 with my family.
wednesday.correctAnswer: went|traveled|visited

wednesday.question: I _____ a few times, but never professionally.
wednesday.correctAnswer: have gone camping|have been camping|have camped

wednesday.question: Translate to English: 'Eu fui a São Paulo no ano passado.' (use Simple Past)
wednesday.correctAnswer: i went to são paulo last year|i went to sao paulo last year|i traveled to são paulo last year

wednesday.question: Translate to English: 'Eu já fui a São Paulo.' (use Present Perfect)
wednesday.correctAnswer: i have been to são paulo|i have been to sao paulo|i've been to são paulo

wednesday.question: Translate to English: 'Quando você foi para a Europa?'
wednesday.correctAnswer: when did you go to europe?

wednesday.question: Which pair of sentences is both correct?
options: I have been there last year. / I went there once.
I went there last year. / I have been there once.
I have went there last year. / I been there once.
I went there last year. / I have gone there once.
wednesday.correctAnswer: I went there last year. / I have been there once.

wednesday.question: Which sentence is wrong?
options: She worked at that company in 2018.
I have tried sushi three times.
He has gone to London last year.
They have never seen snow.
wednesday.correctAnswer: He has gone to London last year.

wednesday.question: What is wrong with this sentence: 'Have you seen that film yesterday?'
options: 'Yesterday' is a specific past time, so we need Simple Past.
The verb 'seen' is wrong — it should be 'saw'.
The auxiliary 'have' is wrong here.
Nothing — the sentence is correct.
wednesday.correctAnswer: 'Yesterday' is a specific past time, so we need Simple Past.

thursday.question: What does 'so far' indicate in 'I have read five chapters so far'?
options: The action has happened up to the present moment and may continue
The action is completely finished
The action happened only once
The action will happen soon
thursday.correctAnswer: The action has happened up to the present moment and may continue

thursday.question: What does 'up to now' mean in context?
options: From the past until the present moment
Only in the future
A long time ago
At a specific moment in the past
thursday.correctAnswer: From the past until the present moment

thursday.question: Which sentence uses 'so far' correctly?
options: I have learned a lot so far in this course.
I learned a lot so far in this course.
I so far have learned a lot in this course.
So far I learned a lot in this course.
thursday.correctAnswer: I have learned a lot so far in this course.

thursday.question: Which sentence uses 'recently' correctly?
options: She has recently started learning guitar.
She recently started learning guitar.
She has started recently learning guitar.
She has started learning guitar recently.
thursday.correctAnswer: She has recently started learning guitar.

thursday.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: He has gained a lot of experience so far.
He gained a lot of experience so far.
He has gain a lot of experience so far.
He have gained a lot of experience so far.
thursday.correctAnswer: He has gained a lot of experience so far.

thursday.question: Which sentence best describes something that has changed up to now?
options: Technology has changed a lot up to now.
Technology changed a lot up to now.
Technology has change a lot up to now.
Technology had changed a lot up to now.
thursday.correctAnswer: Technology has changed a lot up to now.

thursday.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I have recently moved to a new apartment.
I have moved recently to a new apartment.
I recently have moved to a new apartment.
Recently I moved to a new apartment.
thursday.correctAnswer: I have recently moved to a new apartment.

thursday.question: Which question is correct?
options: What have you learned so far?
What did you learn so far?
What you have learned so far?
What have you learned yet?
thursday.correctAnswer: What have you learned so far?

thursday.question: We have completed five lessons _____ _____.
thursday.correctAnswer: so far

thursday.question: She has _____ changed jobs three times. Her career is evolving fast.
thursday.correctAnswer: recently

thursday.question: How many books have you read _____ _____?
thursday.correctAnswer: so far

thursday.question: _____ _____ now, no one has found a perfect solution.
thursday.correctAnswer: up to

thursday.question: I have _____ (meet) many interesting people in this job.
thursday.correctAnswer: met

thursday.question: This year has been very productive. I _____ three new skills so far.
thursday.correctAnswer: have learned|have learned|have picked up|have developed

thursday.question: Up to now, scientists _____ a cure for this disease.
thursday.correctAnswer: have not found|haven't found

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'Até agora, eu aprendi muito neste curso.'
thursday.correctAnswer: so far, i have learned a lot in this course|i have learned a lot in this course so far

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'Ela recentemente começou a praticar yoga.'
thursday.correctAnswer: she has recently started practicing yoga|she has recently started practising yoga

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'Quantos países você visitou até agora?'
thursday.correctAnswer: how many countries have you visited so far?|how many countries have you visited up to now?

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'Até agora, ninguém respondeu minha pergunta.'
thursday.correctAnswer: up to now, no one has answered my question|so far, no one has answered my question

thursday.question: Which sentence is wrong?
options: I have visited five countries so far.
She has recently changed her job.
He has learned a lot up to now.
They so far visited three cities.
thursday.correctAnswer: They so far visited three cities.

friday.question: Which sentence correctly combines Present Perfect and a follow-up Simple Past?
options: I have been to Japan. I went there in 2019.
I have been to Japan. I have gone there in 2019.
I been to Japan. I went there in 2019.
I have went to Japan. I went there in 2019.
friday.correctAnswer: I have been to Japan. I went there in 2019.

friday.question: Which sentence is wrong?
options: I have just finished the report.
She hasn't replied yet.
Have you already heard the news?
He has went to the gym this morning.
friday.correctAnswer: He has went to the gym this morning.

friday.question: A: 'Have you ever done a cooking class?' B: 'Yes! _____ last year with my sister.'
options: I did it
I have done it
I've done it
I had done it
friday.correctAnswer: I did it

friday.question: Which sentence uses the Present Perfect correctly in context?
options: I've seen that documentary — you should watch it too.
I've seen that documentary yesterday.
I've saw that documentary.
I seen that documentary recently.
friday.correctAnswer: I've seen that documentary — you should watch it too.

friday.question: Which is the most natural response to 'Have you ever been skydiving?'
options: No, never. It seems too scary for me.
No, I wasn't.
No, I didn't.
No, I haven't been.
friday.correctAnswer: No, never. It seems too scary for me.

friday.question: Which sentence shows the Present Perfect used correctly with 'so far'?
options: She has completed half of the project so far.
She completed half of the project so far.
She has complete half of the project so far.
She had completed half of the project so far.
friday.correctAnswer: She has completed half of the project so far.

friday.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: He has already eaten, so he isn't hungry.
He already has eaten, so he isn't hungry.
He has eaten already so he isn't hungry yet.
He has yet eaten, so he isn't hungry.
friday.correctAnswer: He has already eaten, so he isn't hungry.

friday.question: Which dialogue uses tenses correctly?
options: A: Have you ever tried surfing? B: Yes! I tried it for the first time in Florianópolis.
A: Have you ever tried surfing? B: Yes! I have tried it for the first time in Florianópolis.
A: Did you ever try surfing? B: Yes! I have tried it in Florianópolis.
A: Have you ever tried surfing? B: Yes! I've tried it last year.
friday.correctAnswer: A: Have you ever tried surfing? B: Yes! I tried it for the first time in Florianópolis.

friday.question: I've _____ (live) in three different cities. Each one taught me something new.
friday.correctAnswer: lived

friday.question: She _____ (just / finish) her degree. She's looking for a job now.
friday.correctAnswer: has just finished

friday.question: Have you _____ read 'The Alchemist'? It's a fantastic book.
friday.correctAnswer: ever

friday.question: We've _____ completed twelve exercises so far. Only eight to go!
friday.correctAnswer: already

friday.question: I _____ (not / see) the new Marvel film _____. Is it good?
friday.correctAnswer: haven't seen yet

friday.question: This is the most challenging project I _____ in my career.
friday.correctAnswer: have ever worked on|have ever done

friday.question: Up to now, my team _____ every single deadline.
friday.correctAnswer: has met|has hit

friday.question: Translate to English: 'Você já fez algo que te assustou muito?'
friday.correctAnswer: have you ever done something that scared you a lot?|have you ever done something really scary?

friday.question: Translate to English: 'Ela acabou de perceber que esqueceu o passaporte.'
friday.correctAnswer: she has just realized she forgot her passport|she's just realized she forgot her passport

friday.question: Translate to English: 'Até agora, esta tem sido a melhor experiência da minha vida.'
friday.correctAnswer: so far, this has been the best experience of my life|up to now, this has been the best experience of my life

friday.question: Translate to English: 'Eu nunca comi comida mexicana, mas adoraria experimentar.'
friday.correctAnswer: i have never eaten mexican food, but i would love to try|i've never eaten mexican food, but i'd love to try

friday.question: Which sentence correctly integrates all key vocabulary and structures from this lesson?
options: I've had many interesting experiences so far, and I've recently decided to share them in a blog.
I had many interesting experiences so far, and I recently decided to share them in a blog.
I've had many interesting experiences so far, and I recently have decided to share them in a blog.
I've had many interesting experiences so far, and I've recently decide to share them in a blog.
friday.correctAnswer: I've had many interesting experiences so far, and I've recently decided to share them in a blog.
